frontier: A385316: smallest N = p³+q³+r³ (primes) in exactly 6 ways — what is a(6)?
action:
Milestone: verifier_construction; contract=sha256:8d51fc61fba0b1e156ad8e3f452b419d28072672bca2b1a6f006cf81817c23cc
Reconstructed the A385316 verifier from focused context (OEIS terms a(1..5), domain/predicate specification). Replayed at C=2×10¹⁰: domain kill fixtures (2/2 reject composite-injected triples), predicate kill fixtures (3/3 reject non-multiplicity claims), and all five OEIS terms reproduced exactly.
verified:
Verifier domain: P = {p prime : p^3 <= C}; enumerate nondecreasing triples (p<=q<=r) with p^3+q^3+r^3 <= C.
Verifier predicate: count[s] = #{(p,q,r) : p<=q<=r, p³+q³+r³=s}; must equal n for a(n).
Domain kill fixtures: 2/2 rejected correctly
Predicate kill fixtures: 3/3 rejected correctly

result: Verifier construction milestone fully replayed and verified locally. All five OEIS terms reproduced at C=2×10¹⁰. Kill fixtures pass with full branch coverage (domain: composite rejection + empty-set correctness; predicate: multiplicity mismatch rejection for three known terms). No k=6 candidate found up to C=2×10¹⁰ (the accepted continuation's bound). The discriminating bound a(6) > 10¹³ requires DGX SSH access, not available from this Mac.
next_gate: Operator with DGX SSH access must run the same verifier at C=10¹³. If a k=6 candidate appears, report it with certificate; if not, certify a(6) > 10¹³ and advance to C=10¹⁴ or a heuristic/construction approach.
